up.method: D x 2.2 mM (all cellobiose consumed by C.c.)

sec.units: mol per mol cellobiose (2.7 in supernatant)

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: end products digestible by other trophic groups

growth_records.cite: Miller, Lance D et al. Establishment and metabolic analysis of a model microbial community for understanding trophic and electron accepting interactions of subsurface anaerobic environments. BMC Microbiology (2010). DOI:10.1186/1471-2180-10-149. [PMC2906461]
growth_records.snippet: C. cellulolyticum was likely growth limited by the availability of cellobiose... produced little to no lactate... Each mole of cellobiose led to 2.7 moles acetate in the supernatant
growth_records.notes: Dominant fermenter in anaerobic tri-culture chemostat. mu=D=0.03/h at steady state (cellobiose-limited); doubling=23.1 h. Cellobiose (2.2 mM feed) fully consumed by C.c.: volumetric 0.03x2.2=0.066 mmol/L/h; per-gDW using modeled C.c. biomass 210 mg/L (Table 2): 0.066/0.210=0.314 mmol/gDW/h. Products 2.7 mol acetate + ~equimolar CO2 per mol cellobiose (Fig 5 model). H2 abundant in pure batch but <0.1 mM in tri-culture.
